agentFile.format: Markdown file with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- fences
agentFile.bodyIs: Markdown body below the frontmatter is the agent's system prompt / instructions (max 30,000 characters for cloud agent). Tools referenced with #tool:<name>; content is prepended to user prompts when the agent activates.

name.description: Display name for the custom agent; defaults to the filename if omitted.

description.description: Outlines the agent's purpose and capabilities. Required for Copilot cloud agent; in VS Code shown as placeholder text in the chat input field.

tools.allowed: tool names, tool set names, MCP tools, extension tools, ["*"], or []
tools.description: Controls which tools the agent can use; defaults to all tools if unset.

agents.description: List of agent names available as subagents in this agent (VS Code).

model.allowed: a single model name (e.g. 'Claude Sonnet 4', 'GPT-4.1') or a prioritized array (e.g. ['Claude Opus 4.5','GPT-5.2'])
model.default: user's model picker selection / inherited default
model.description: Model used to run the agent. Array form = try models in order (VS Code/IDEs). Array syntax is rejected by Copilot CLI (single string only).

target.description: Restricts the agent to a specific environment; available in both if unset.

disable-model-invocation.description: When true, prevents the agent from being automatically invoked as a subagent by other agents.

user-invocable.description: Controls whether the agent appears in the agents dropdown for manual selection.

mcp-servers.allowed: MCP server configuration objects (type, command, args, tools, env)
mcp-servers.description: Additional MCP servers and tools for the agent. Used by Copilot cloud agent; not used in VS Code/IDE custom agents.

handoffs.allowed: handoff objects with label (required), agent (required), prompt, send (bool), model
handoffs.description: Suggested next actions/prompts to transition between agents (VS Code). Not supported for Copilot cloud agent.

argument-hint.description: Hint text shown in the chat input field to guide users (VS Code). Not supported for Copilot cloud agent.

hooks.description: Hook commands scoped to this agent (VS Code, Preview feature).

metadata.description: Enables agent annotation; not used in VS Code or IDE agents.

infer.description: Retired. Replaced by disable-model-invocation and user-invocable.

bashInjection.description: No documented mechanism for executing shell commands inline within an agent/prompt body to inject their stdout into the prompt. Command execution happens via agent tools (e.g. runCommands), not via in-content templating. unverified-absence.

fileInjection.description: File references are expressed as relative-path Markdown links in the prompt/agent body; the linked file content is provided as context.

argumentTokens.description: Prompt files accept user-supplied input variables. ${input:name} substitutes a named argument; the optional :placeholder form shows placeholder text. Scope: prompt files.

toolReference.description: References a specific tool from within the agent/prompt/instructions Markdown body so it is invoked or made available. Scope: agent, prompt, instructions.

mcpReference.token: frontmatter 'mcp-servers' (agent files) / JSON 'mcpServers' (cloud repo settings & VS Code mcp.json). Server tools referenced as <server-name>/* or by tool name. Secrets via $VAR / ${VAR} / ${VAR:-default} with required COPILOT_MCP_ prefix.
mcpReference.description: MCP servers extend an agent's tools. Cloud JSON schema: each server has type (local|stdio|http|sse, required), tools (string[] or ["*"], required), command/args/env (local|stdio), url/headers (http|sse). Secret/env references must be prefixed COPILOT_MCP_ and support $VAR, ${VAR}, and ${VAR:-default_value} substitution. Scope: agent (tooling).

variableTemplating.description: Prompt-file body templating: ${input:...} for user arguments and ${selection} for the current editor selection. Scope: prompt files.

agentMention.token: frontmatter 'agents: [name, ...]' (subagents); prompt files select an agent via 'agent: <name>'; agents invoked in chat with the agents dropdown / #tool:agent
agentMention.description: An agent can declare callable subagents via the 'agents' frontmatter array; prompt files target an agent via the 'agent' field. No documented inline @-mention token for agents in body text. unverified for inline mention syntax.

applyToGlobs.description: In .github/instructions/*.instructions.md, the applyTo glob (comma-separated patterns) auto-attaches the instruction file to matching files. Required per GitHub Docs; optional per VS Code (without it, manual attachment only). Multiple matching files stack (union).
